metadata:
Nies Mapping Group, Inc1993UnknownRedmondGIS.DBO.PavementEdgeFirstvector digital data SMMS_Citation_Linkages_Hyperlinked: false SMMS_Citation_Source_Time_Period: RedmondGIS.DBO.PavementEdgeServer=GeminiDB; Service=sde:sqlserver:GeminiDB; Database=RedmondGIS; Version=dbo.DEFAULTThe datasets provides a geographic location of edge of the pavement within the City of Redmond city limits. The edge of the pavement typically represents edges of paved roads and parking lots. The location of the edges of the pavements was derived from survey information, field verification, digitized based on an aerial photography, paper and electronic record drawings, and/or construction drawings. The mapping of the pavement originated from 1993 aerial photography project identified during the Sewer Comprehensive Plan update. Furthermore, separate contracts were initiated with Triathlon Ltd. (formally Nies Mapping Group, Inc.) to update the datasets from 1996 and 1999 aerial photos. The original data was delivered in Microstation (CAD) format and later imported into GeoMedia/MS Access warehouseThe purpose of the data set is to identify location of all paved roads and parking lots within the City of Redmond. The dataset provides a foundation for mapping water, wastewater, and stormwater infrastructure as well as for transportation related projects. The dataset also provides support for fire and emergency medical care response mapping and analysis such as impervious areas analysisen19930414unknownPresentunknownground conditionIn workAs needed-127.484702-121.52354347.74588646.8082970.0000001468163.4618710.000000272859.754753RedmondPavementRoadParkingExistingInfrastructureLotImperviousAll access to the dataset will be as Read-Only.Distribution to the consultant and customers should be processed through the GIS Services Group, Finance and Information Services department. All requestors are required to fill a request form and sign disclaimer statementmailing and physical address15670 NE 85th ST, MS:3SFNRedmondWA98073USA(425) 556-2134(425) 556-4200!_gisservices@redmond.govMonday-Friday 9AM to 5PM SMMS_Contact_Email_Hyperlinked: true City of Redmond, GIS ServicesSMMS_ID_Browse_Graphic_Hyperlinked: 0Microsoft Windows XP Version 5.1 (Build 2600) Service Pack 3; ESRI ArcCatalog 9.3.1.4000Nies Mapping Group, Inc. Bellevue, WA (Nies Mapping Inc. was later purchased by Triathlon Ltd.)1993Unknown1993 Aerial OrthophotographyThe Water and Sewer Facilities Management Inventory project was initiated in March, 1993 by the Water and Sewer Utilities division of the Public Works Department. The date of photos was 04/14/1993. The planimetric (digital) maps were delivered in Microstation DGN (CAD) format. These CAD files were delivered as quarter sections, with and overlap, in the 1 inch = 100 feet scale. The project included the following features; pre-marked survey monument cases, roads (paved and gravel), trails and paths, parking lots, tanks and reservoirs, water vaults, water valves, water meters, fire hydrants, sewer manholes and cleanouts, sewer pump stations, lake, ponds, streams, and building (commercial, industrial, and multi-family) outlines. In 1995 the Public Works Stormwater division and the Planning department awarded a second contract to Nies Mapping group. The second contract included the delivery of aerial photos (black and white 4 inch pixel resolution and color 1 ft, 2 ft, and 4 ft pixel resolution photos). Additionally 2 and 10 foot contours were also delivered. The photos were delivered in TIFF format. The contours were delivered in AutoCAD (CAD) format SMMS_Citation_Linkages_Hyperlinked: false SMMS_Citation_Source_Time_Period: SMMS_Citation_Source_Currentness: publication date Triathlon Ltd. Richmond, BC1999Unknown1999 Aerial OrthophotographyThe City of Redmond only purchased the license to use the aerial orthophotos. The Triathlon Ltd owns all rights, title and interest, including copyright of the photos. Pixel resolution of the photos is 0.5 ft and the photo scale 1 inch = 600 feet. 86 images were mosaicked to produce 3000 ft x 3000ft tiles. The images were delivered in uncompressed TIFF version 6 format. The images were later compressed to MrSID format by city staff. Files In 1999 the Public Works Department awarded a contract to Triathlon Ltd. to update the following digital layers; building (commercial, industrial, and multi-family) outlines, edge of pavement, pavement markings, and parking lots. These files were delivered in Microstation (CAD) dgn format SMMS_Citation_Linkages_Hyperlinked: false SMMS_Citation_Source_Scale: 600 SMMS_Citation_Source_Time_Period: Nies Mapping Group, Inc. Bellevue, WA (Nies Mapping Inc. was later purchased by Triathlon Ltd.)1996Unknown1996 Aerial OrthophotographyThe photos were purchased directly from Nies Mapping Group, Inc. Nies Mapping conducted photogrammetric project of the Puget Sound Region (1800 square mile project area) for the Washington State Department of Transportation project. The orthophotos pixel resolution is 1 meter. The photos were created in metric projection and un-usable to the City of Redmond. Therefore, The City of Redmond Public Works Department contracted Nies Mapping to convert the photos to survey feet projection. The photo scale is 1 inch = 2500 feet. Files were delivered in TIFF format SMMS_Citation_Linkages_Hyperlinked: false SMMS_Citation_Source_Time_Period:
SMMS_Type: 1 SMMS_Dataset_Capture_Browse: true SMMS_Dataset: SMMS_Dataset_NorthArrow: 0 SMMS_Dataset_Legand: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer: SMMS_DatasetLayer_Order: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Type: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Location: \SID\GISUSER\geographic_information\Data\Infrastructure\PavementEdge.mdb SMMS_DatasetLayer_Name: PavementEdge SMMS_DatasetLayer_LabelVisible: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Style1: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Color: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Weight: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer: SMMS_DatasetLayer_Order: 1 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Type: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Location: \BUZZ\GISUSER\geographic_information\Data\LandBase\MasterParcel.mdb SMMS_DatasetLayer_Name: RightOfWay SMMS_DatasetLayer_LabelVisible: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Style1: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Color: 0 SMMS_DatasetLayer_Weight: 0 SDE Feature ClassnonenonenonenonenoneNies Mapping Group, Inc. Bellevue, WA (Nies Mapping Inc. was later purchased by Triathlon Ltd.)1993Unknown1993 Aerial OrthophotographyThe Water and Sewer Facilities Management Inventory project was initiated in March, 1993 by the Water and Sewer Utilities division of the Public Works Department. The date of photos was 04/14/1993. The planimetric (digital) maps were delivered in Microstation DGN (CAD) format. These CAD files were delivered as quarter sections, with and overlap, in the 1 inch = 100 feet scale. The project included the following features; pre-marked survey monument cases, roads (paved and gravel), trails and paths, parking lots, tanks and reservoirs, water vaults, water valves, water meters, fire hydrants, sewer manholes and cleanouts, sewer pump stations, lake, ponds, streams, and building (commercial, industrial, and multi-family) outlines. In 1995 the Public Works Stormwater division and the Planning department awarded a second contract to Nies Mapping group. The second contract included the delivery of aerial photos (black and white 4 inch pixel resolution and color 1 ft, 2 ft, and 4 ft pixel resolution photos). Additionally 2 and 10 foot contours were also delivered. The photos were delivered in TIFF format. The contours were delivered in AutoCAD (CAD) format SMMS_Citation_Linkages_Hyperlinked: false SMMS_Citation_Source_Time_Period: SMMS_Citation_Source_Currentness: publication date unknownpublication date1993 Aerial OrthophotographyNies Mapping Group, Inc. Bellevue, WA (Nies Mapping Inc. was later purchased by Triathlon Ltd.)1996Unknown1996 Aerial OrthophotographyThe photos were purchased directly from Nies Mapping Group, Inc. Nies Mapping conducted photogrammetric project of the Puget Sound Region (1800 square mile project area) for the Washington State Department of Transportation project. The orthophotos pixel resolution is 1 meter. The photos were created in metric projection and un-usable to the City of Redmond. Therefore, The City of Redmond Public Works Department contracted Nies Mapping to convert the photos to survey feet projection. The photo scale is 1 inch = 2500 feet. Files were delivered in TIFF format SMMS_Citation_Linkages_Hyperlinked: false SMMS_Citation_Source_Time_Period: Unknown1996 Aerial OrthophotographyTriathlon Ltd. Richmond, BC1999Unknown1999 Aerial OrthophotographyThe City of Redmond only purchased the license to use the aerial orthophotos. The Triathlon Ltd owns all rights, title and interest, including copyright of the photos. Pixel resolution of the photos is 0.5 ft and the photo scale 1 inch = 600 feet. 86 images were mosaicked to produce 3000 ft x 3000ft tiles. The images were delivered in uncompressed TIFF version 6 format. The images were later compressed to MrSID format by city staff. Files In 1999 the Public Works Department awarded a contract to Triathlon Ltd. to update the following digital layers; building (commercial, industrial, and multi-family) outlines, edge of pavement, pavement markings, and parking lots. These files were delivered in Microstation (CAD) dgn format SMMS_Citation_Linkages_Hyperlinked: false SMMS_Citation_Source_Scale: 600 SMMS_Citation_Source_Time_Period: 600Unknown1999 Aerial OrthophotographyEdge of pavement segments were collected from the aerial photos using specialized photogrametric software by the consultant. Consultant imported the data into Microstation version 4 dgn formatUnknownmailing and physical address15670 NE 85th ST, MS:3FSNRedmondWA98073USA(425) 556-2134(425) 556-4200!_gisservices@redmond.govMonday-Friday 9AM to 5PM SMMS_Contact_Email_Hyperlinked: true City of Redmond, GIS ServicesPavement segments were imported from Microstation to GeoMedia MS Access 97 warehouse. The process was accomplished through CSD (Cad Server Schema Description) method of GeoMedia Professional version 3.0. New database schema was createdUnknownmailing and physical address15670 NE 85th ST, MS:3FSNRedmondWA98073USA(425) 556-2134(425) 556-4200!_gisservices@redmond.govMonday-Friday 9AM to 5PM SMMS_Contact_Email_Hyperlinked: true City of Redmond, GIS ServicesDigitized from scanned drawings and/or electronic construction/record drawingsmailing and physical address15670 NE 85th ST, MS:3FSNRedmondWA98073USA(425) 556-2134(425) 556-4200!_gisservices@redmond.govMonday-Friday 9AM to 5PM SMMS_Contact_Email_Hyperlinked: true City of Redmond, GIS ServicesMetadata imported.C:\DOCUME~1\xqin\LOCALS~1\Temp\xmlF8.tmp2008011415511600Update Pavement Edge Feature Class with new pavement configurations shown on 2007 King County Aerial Photography. The project limited itself to obvious major pavement edge configuration changes. Minor mismatches were not addressed -less than 2 feet. The critical areas addressed were along Redmond Way, Downtown Redmond, new SR520 overpass, Bear Creek Area including parking lots. The project worked in conjunction with the publication of the new fire map books.
Toplogical errors were fixed in these critical areas.12/31/2008UnknownVector SMMS_Captured: true SimpleFALSE0FALSEFALSEcoordinate pairsurvey feet0.0000000.000000D_North_American_1983_HARNGeodetic Reference System 806378137.000000298.257222GCS_North_American_1983_HARNNAD_1983_HARN_StatePlane_Washington_North_FIPS_4601_FeetNorth American Vertical Datum of 19881.000000survey feetExplicit elevation coordinate included with horizontal coordinatesPavementEdgeEdge geometry featureGIS ServicesFeature Class0FIDFIDOID400Internal feature number.EsriSequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.CommentsField for various commentsGIS ServicesAs needed SMMS_Captured: true CommentsString10000ProjectNamProjectNamString5000RecordDrawRecordDrawString2000RecordDr_1RecordDr_1String2000RecordDr_2RecordDr_2String2000d_Ownershid_OwnershiString600d_DataSourd_DataSourString600d_CategoryIdentifies the type of the pavement edge curb, driveway, etc. as identified in the Standard Details handbookGIS ServicesEPEdge of Pavement (fog line)FCFace of Curb (unknown type)FC_AFace of Type A CurbFC_A1Face of Type A1 CurbFC_RFace of Rolled CurbDWDriveway curb cut (unknown type)DW_1Driveway Type 1DW_1ADriveway Type 1ADW_2Driveway Type 2DW_3Driveway Type 3DW_CRCurb Return DrivewayFC_EBFace of Extruded or Barrier CurbUNKUnknownAs needed SMMS_Captured: true d_CategoryString600ShapeShapeGeometry000Feature geometry.ESRICoordinates defining the features.CreatedByCreatedByString5000ArcGIS editor tracking field. System generated username of who created the feature.DateCreateDateCreateDate800ModifiedByModifiedByString5000ArcGIS Editor Tracking field. System-generated username of who most recently modified the featureDateModifiDateModifiDate800AssetIDAssetIDString3000Unique ID across entire GIS databaseLucityAutoLucityAutoInteger10100d_AssetTypd_AssetTypString3000GlobalIDGlobalIDString3800Unique identification automatically calculated by ArcGIS and used with geodatabase replicationShape_STLeShape_STLeDouble1900This feature identifies edges of paved areas (roads, parking lots, driveways, etc.) within the City of Redmond city limits. The attributes specify the source of the graphical information, the name of the user who created the information, the type of the curb if any, record drawing number, and other informationGIS Services, Public Works Transportation Divisionmailing and physical address15670 NE 85th ST, MS:3SFNRedmondWA98073USA(425) 556-2134(425) 556-4200!_gisservices@redmond.govMonday-Friday 9AM to 5PM Connections\RedmondGIS.sde\RedmondGIS.DBO.Transportation\RedmondGIS.DBO.PavementEdge" CreatedBy DateCreated ModifiedBy DateModified ADD_FIELDS DATABASE_TIMECalculateField GeminiDB\PavementEdge RedPavementEdgeID autoIncrement() PYTHON_9.3 "rec=0
def autoIncrement():
 global rec
 pStart = 1
 pInterval = 1
 if (rec == 0): 
 rec = pStart 
 else: 
 rec = rec + pInterval 
 return rec

"CalculateField GeminiDB\PavementEdge d_AssetType "PavementEdge" VB #CalculateField GeminiDB\PavementEdge AssetID ""trPe" &""& [RedPavementEdgeID]" VB #CalculateField RedmondGIS.DBO.PavementEdge AssetID ""trPe" & [RedPavementEdgeID]" VB #CalculateField RedmondGIS.DBO.PavementEdge RedPavementEdgeID autoIncrement() PYTHON_9.3 "rec=0
def autoIncrement():

 pInterval = 1

 rec = pStart 
 else: 
 rec = rec + pInterval 
 return rec

 return rec

(0.304801)NAD_1983_HARN_StatePlane_Washington_North_FIPS_4601_Feet<ProjectedCoordinateSystem xsi:type='typens:ProjectedCoordinateSystem' xmlns:xsi='http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance' xmlns:xs='http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema' xmlns:typens='http://www.esri.com/schemas/ArcGIS/3.3.0'><WKT>PROJCS["NAD_1983_HARN_StatePlane_Washington_North_FIPS_4601_Feet",GEOGCS["GCS_North_American_1983_HARN",DATUM["D_North_American_1983_HARN",SPHEROID["GRS_1980",6378137.0,298.257222101]],PRIMEM["Greenwich",0.0],UNIT["Degree",0.0174532925199433]],PROJECTION["Lambert_Conformal_Conic"],PARAMETER["False_Easting",1640416.666666667],PARAMETER["False_Northing",0.0],PARAMETER["Central_Meridian",-120.8333333333333],PARAMETER["Standard_Parallel_1",47.5],PARAMETER["Standard_Parallel_2",48.73333333333333],PARAMETER["Latitude_Of_Origin",47.0],UNIT["Foot_US",0.3048006096012192],AUTHORITY["EPSG",2926]],VERTCS["NGVD_1929",VDATUM["National_Geodetic_Vertical_Datum_1929"],PARAMETER["Vertical_Shift",0.0],PARAMETER["Direction",1.0],UNIT["Foot_US",0.3048006096012192],AUTHORITY["EPSG",5702]]</WKT><XOrigin>-117104300</XOrigin><YOrigin>-99539600</YOrigin><XYScale>37926742.22403606</XYScale><ZOrigin>-100000</ZOrigin><ZScale>3048.0060960121918</ZScale><MOrigin>-100000</MOrigin><MScale>10000</MScale><XYTolerance>0.0032808333333333331</XYTolerance><ZTolerance>0.0032808333333333331</ZTolerance><MTolerance>0.001</MTolerance><HighPrecision>true</HighPrecision><WKID>2926</WKID><LatestWKID>2926</LatestWKID><VCSWKID>5702</VCSWKID><LatestVCSWKID>5702</LatestVCSWKID></ProjectedCoordinateSystem>1.0Item Description1500000005000FGDCMicrosoft Windows Server 2016 Technical Preview Version 10.0 (Build 20348) ; Esri ArcGIS 13.3.0.51575PavementEdge01468163.461871272859.75475301-127.484702-121.52354347.74588646.80829711-122.170640-121.95920747.72669047.617350The purpose of the data set is to identify location of all paved roads and parking lots within the City of Redmond. The dataset provides a foundation for mapping water, wastewater, and stormwater infrastructure as well as for transportation related projects. The dataset also provides support for fire and emergency medical care response mapping and analysis such as impervious areas analysis<DIV STYLE="text-align:Left;"><DIV><DIV><P><SPAN>The datasets provides a geographic location of edge of the pavement within the City of Redmond city limits. The edge of the pavement typically represents edges of paved roads and parking lots. The location of the edges of the pavements was derived from survey information, field verification, digitized based on an aerial photography, paper and electronic record drawings, and/or construction drawings. The mapping of the pavement originated from 1993 aerial photography project identified during the Sewer Comprehensive Plan update. Furthermore, separate contracts were initiated with Triathlon Ltd. (formally Nies Mapping Group, Inc.) to update the datasets from 1996 and 1999 aerial photos. The original data was delivered in Microstation (CAD) format and later imported into GeoMedia/MS Access warehouse</SPAN></P></DIV></DIV></DIV>PavementRoadParkingExistingInfrastructureLotImpervious<DIV STYLE="text-align:Left;"><DIV><DIV><P STYLE="margin:0 0 11 0;"><SPAN><SPAN>While the data have been tested for accuracy and are properly functioning, the City of Redmond disclaims any responsibility for the accuracy or correctness of the data.</SPAN></SPAN></P><P STYLE="margin:0 0 11 0;"><SPAN><SPAN>The foregoing warranty is exclusive and in lieu of all other warranties of merchantability, fitness for particular purpose and/or any other type whether expressed or implied.</SPAN></SPAN></P><P STYLE="margin:0 0 11 0;"><SPAN><SPAN>In no event shall the City of Redmond become liable to users of these data, or any other party, for any loss or damages, consequential or otherwise, including but not limited to time, money, or goodwill, arising from the use, operation or modification of the data. In using these data, users further agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless City of Redmond for any and all liability of any nature arising out of or resulting from the lack of accuracy or correctness of the data, or the use of the data.</SPAN></SPAN></P><P STYLE="margin:0 0 11 0;"><SPAN><SPAN>Please acknowledge the City of Redmond as a source when City of Redmond data are used in the preparation of reports, papers, publications, maps, and other products.</SPAN></SPAN></P><P STYLE="margin:0 0 11 0;"><SPAN>To ensure that appropriate documentation and data limitations are provided, these databases should not be redistributed to any other parties.</SPAN></P></DIV></DIV></DIV>ISO 19115 Geographic Information - MetadataDIS_ESRI1.0dataset002Server=GeminiDB; Service=sde:sqlserver:GeminiDB; Database=RedmondGIS; Version=dbo.DEFAULTArcSDE ConnectionSDE Feature ClassShapefile0.000NAD_1983_HARN_StatePlane_Washington_North_FIPS_4601_FeetEPSG6.3(9.0.0)020260602
